caption.text:
So, you're thinking of breaking up with ChatGPT? You're not alone.

A major ethical divide just split the AI world in two, and users are voting with their feet (and their downloads).

This isn't just about features anymore—it's about principles. After Anthropic (the company behind Claude) took a hard stand against using their AI for military surveillance, OpenAI went the other way, and the user base noticed.

Claude is now the #1 free app, and the exodus from ChatGPT is real.

If you're considering the switch, don't leave your data behind. This guide walks you through the entire process: exporting your history from ChatGPT, importing it seamlessly into Claude, and—most importantly—permanently deleting your old account to protect your privacy.

Ready to make a clean break? Let's do it right.

#AI #ClaudeAI #ChatGPT #TechEthics #DataPrivacy

carousel_media.accessibility_caption: Photo by Rachel |  Building businesses that don’t suck on March 03, 2026. May be a Twitter screenshot of poster and text that says "rachelgronback Helping a client setup their Claude as an accountability partner (who swears occasionally, has good jokes, but also doesn't let them get away with shit) and these are the basic settings would add to any new account: -Avoid contrast framing (e.g. "this isn't X, it's Y"). Just say what something is. -Avoid engagement bait, false suspense hooks, and contrarian hooks (e.g. "but here's the thing," "here' 's what most people get wrong," "the real question is"). Just say it. 1/3 1/3 5 likes 5 replies".

Photo by Rachel |  Building businesses that don’t suck on March 03, 2026. May be an illustration of poster and text that says "rachelgronback Replying to @rachelgronback ර -No em dashes. Use commas, full stops, or restructure the sentence. -Use UK English spelling and conventions -Don't overuse ellipses. Use them sparingly for genuine trailing off -No therapy speak. Don't say things like "that sounds really hard," "| hear you,", "let's unpack that," "you're not broken". Just talk like a person. -Don't assume need comforting If I'm working through something, match my energy. want a thinking partner, not a support hotline. 2/3 2/3 2 replies".

Photo by Rachel |  Building businesses that don’t suck on March 03, 2026. May be an illustration of poster and text that says "rachelgronback Replying to @rachelgronback ර And for my easily distracted ADHD fam: -Don't immediately offer to create resources, documents, or frameworks. Assume ' still thinking. Stay in the conversation help me work through my Ba idea until ask you to build something. Keep me on track. If I'm drifting from the original topic, flag it. Say something like "we started on X, do you want to keep going here or come back to that?" 3/3 3/3 2 replies".

caption.text:
People spend way too much time on prompts, focus on setup instead. In Claude this exists in four main spots - your user preferences, project setup, memories and skills. 

User preferences = all the time 
Project setup = only this project 
Memories = all the time context but only updated once a day so tomorrow’s memory will be full of today’s work 
Skills = you choose when this knowledge is triggered…all the time or at specific moments. 

And if you need more than this, you create a knowledge base in something like Obsidian or Notion and connect it up. 

#claude #ai #aiforwomen #chatgpt
